title: Hamnet Was the Biggest Winner of the 2026 Golden Globes Weekend
text: Hamnet emerged as the clear winner of the 2026 Golden Globes weekend, taking home multiple major awards including Best Picture Drama and Best Actress for Jessie Buckley. The Chloé Zhao-directed adaptation of Maggie O'Farrell's novel demonstrated remarkable strength across the awards landscape. The film's first victory came with Jessie Buckley winning Best Actress in a Motion Picture Drama, a well-deserved recognition of her powerful portrayal of Agnes Shakespeare. The second and final award came with the film's triumph in Best Picture Drama, clinching the ceremony's last presented award. Buckley's win marked the culmination of an impressive run that began when the film debuted at the Telluride Film Festival to critical acclaim. Her performance was consistently praised as one of the strongest of the awards season. The film's momentum continued through the Critics Choice Awards and BAFTA Awards before reaching its peak at the Golden Globes. Industry observers noted that Hamnet's success positioned the film and Buckley favorably for the upcoming Academy Awards, with expectations for further recognition during the March ceremony.
